texts.orig: PV and thermally driven small-scale, stand-alone solar desalination systems with very low maintenance needs

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Abstract
texts.text: Abstract

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This paper presents the thermal performance of a membrane distillation (MD) solar desalination unit located at the Mechanical Engineering Department site, Alexandria University, Alexandria, Egypt. The unit is designed and installed as a part of a partially funded European Commission (EC) project named 'PV and thermally-driven smallscale, stand-alone desalination system with very low maintenance needs (SMADES)'. The basic innovative MD principle and module is highlighted. The Alexandria MD unit is described and the unit performance is presented for the 6 months of its operation period. The unit performance covers the transient changes in the unit productivity, unit feed water, brine and product water temperatures and conductivities, unit salt rejection, solar collector and MD process efficiencies. The unit performance, in clear and cloudy days, for typical summer and winter months, is presented. For a sunny day of, say, 7.25 kWh/d the results indicate that the unit produces about 11.2 l/d for every m 2 of the collector area. The relatively high productivity, above that of the conventional solar still, is due to the partial recovery of the condensation energy. The overall unit productivity has been correlated against the solar irradiation as: Daily production (l/m 2 .d) = 1.666 × Daily radiation (kWh/m 2 .d) - 1.67.
texts.text: This paper presents the thermal performance of a membrane distillation (MD) solar desalination unit located at the Mechanical Engineering Department site, Alexandria University, Alexandria, Egypt. The unit is designed and installed as a part of a partially funded European Commission (EC) project named 'PV and thermally-driven smallscale, stand-alone desalination system with very low maintenance needs (SMADES)'. The basic innovative MD principle and module is highlighted. The Alexandria MD unit is described and the unit performance is presented for the 6 months of its operation period. The unit performance covers the transient changes in the unit productivity, unit feed water, brine and product water temperatures and conductivities, unit salt rejection, solar collector and MD process efficiencies. The unit performance, in clear and cloudy days, for typical summer and winter months, is presented. For a sunny day of, say, 7.25 kWh/d the results indicate that the unit produces about 11.2 l/d for every m 2 of the collector area. The relatively high productivity, above that of the conventional solar still, is due to the partial recovery of the condensation energy. The overall unit productivity has been correlated against the solar irradiation as: Daily production (l/m 2 .d) = 1.666 × Daily radiation (kWh/m 2 .d) - 1.67.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: became an international problem confronting human groups and countries. The problem is more apparent in the Arab and Middle East/North Africa (MENA) countries due to very limited natural resources of fresh water.
texts.text: became an international problem confronting human groups and countries. The problem is more apparent in the Arab and Middle East/North Africa (MENA) countries due to very limited natural resources of fresh water.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Desalination (desalting) has been presented for decades as a suitable alternative for partial solution of the world fresh water crisis. Desalination of brackish and seawater can provide the need of drinking water without any serious impact on the environment. As a result, there has been a dramatic world wide increase in the number and capacity of desalination plants [1].  Although it seems more expensive in areas with surface or groundwater availability, it is not so in areas 300-500 km far from fresh water resources. For large water production, conventional desalination technologies as MSF, MED, RO, VC, etc. have been proven to be technically and economically suitable. However, for (i) small communities of limited fresh water demand (up to 10 m 3 /d), (ii) areas far from the sources of water and energy (fuel and electricity), and (iii) communities of small technical capabilities, solar desalination is more applicable.
texts.text: Desalination (desalting) has been presented for decades as a suitable alternative for partial solution of the world fresh water crisis. Desalination of brackish and seawater can provide the need of drinking water without any serious impact on the environment. As a result, there has been a dramatic world wide increase in the number and capacity of desalination plants [1].  Although it seems more expensive in areas with surface or groundwater availability, it is not so in areas 300-500 km far from fresh water resources. For large water production, conventional desalination technologies as MSF, MED, RO, VC, etc. have been proven to be technically and economically suitable. However, for (i) small communities of limited fresh water demand (up to 10 m 3 /d), (ii) areas far from the sources of water and energy (fuel and electricity), and (iii) communities of small technical capabilities, solar desalination is more applicable.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Solar desalination in many respects might be an ideal solution to small communities in many MENA countries, as shown by Malik et al. [2] and Fath [3], due to the following facts:
texts.text: Solar desalination in many respects might be an ideal solution to small communities in many MENA countries, as shown by Malik et al. [2] and Fath [3], due to the following facts:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Many of these countries enjoy an abundant solar intensity (annual daily average is between 200-300 W/m 2 ) and large annual sun hours (3000-5000 h/y), and therefore incident energy of about 5-8 kWh/m 2  d.
texts.text: Many of these countries enjoy an abundant solar intensity (annual daily average is between 200-300 W/m 2 ) and large annual sun hours (3000-5000 h/y), and therefore incident energy of about 5-8 kWh/m 2  d.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · The diurnal and seasonal fluctuations in solar desalination productivity are intrinsically linked to the fluctuating water demand.
texts.text: The diurnal and seasonal fluctuations in solar desalination productivity are intrinsically linked to the fluctuating water demand.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Solar energy is available in almost every location and, in addition, is an environment friendly energy resource (with no CO 2 emission).
texts.text: Solar energy is available in almost every location and, in addition, is an environment friendly energy resource (with no CO 2 emission).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Solar desalination can be divided into direct and indirect technologies. In the direct methods, the solar energy collector and desalination component are an integral unit, e.g. solar stills [4-
texts.text: Solar desalination can be divided into direct and indirect technologies. In the direct methods, the solar energy collector and desalination component are an integral unit, e.g. solar stills [4-

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 10].  A general rule of thumb for simple solar stills is  that  a  solar  collection  area  of  about  1  m 2 is needed to produce 3-5 l of water per day. Thus, for a 1- and 10-m 3 /d facility, a land area of about 250 and 2500 m 2  would be required, respectively. These areas may even be doubled to allow for spacing. Thus, a large solar collection area is required with the resultant high capital costs. To improve the performance of solar stills, multi-effect solar stills were used. In the indirect methods, solar energy is first collected, converted to usable heat or electricity and then it is used as an energy source for the different desalination technologies. Many researchers have investigated and developed different indirect  solar  desalination systems, PV-RO, and solar thermal-MEE units, etc., [11-15]. Each of these indirect solar desalination systems has its positive and negative features and none of them has proved to be the best either technically or economically.
texts.text: 10].  A general rule of thumb for simple solar stills is  that  a  solar  collection  area  of  about  1  m 2 is needed to produce 3-5 l of water per day. Thus, for a 1- and 10-m 3 /d facility, a land area of about 250 and 2500 m 2  would be required, respectively. These areas may even be doubled to allow for spacing. Thus, a large solar collection area is required with the resultant high capital costs. To improve the performance of solar stills, multi-effect solar stills were used. In the indirect methods, solar energy is first collected, converted to usable heat or electricity and then it is used as an energy source for the different desalination technologies. Many researchers have investigated and developed different indirect  solar  desalination systems, PV-RO, and solar thermal-MEE units, etc., [11-15]. Each of these indirect solar desalination systems has its positive and negative features and none of them has proved to be the best either technically or economically.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. SMADES project
texts.text: 2. SMADES project

texts.label: text
texts.orig: SMADES is project in solar desalination partially funded by the EC. The overall objective of the SMADES project is the development of standalone desalination systems for arid and semi-arid remote regions with lack of drinking water but high solar irradiation. The modular system set-up is based on the highly innovative membrane distillation (MD) technology. The system integrates both solar thermal and solar PV energies. The desalination energy is supplied entirely by solar thermal collectors and the electrical auxiliary energy is supplied by PV.
texts.text: SMADES is project in solar desalination partially funded by the EC. The overall objective of the SMADES project is the development of standalone desalination systems for arid and semi-arid remote regions with lack of drinking water but high solar irradiation. The modular system set-up is based on the highly innovative membrane distillation (MD) technology. The system integrates both solar thermal and solar PV energies. The desalination energy is supplied entirely by solar thermal collectors and the electrical auxiliary energy is supplied by PV.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: To gather a detailed present state of technology and problems on small-scale stand-alone desalination plants, a technical study was carried out in the beginning of the project. A second study, after reasonable developments, has been carried out to discover the need and the social impact for potential users and to focus on the legal frame works and economic influences.
texts.text: To gather a detailed present state of technology and problems on small-scale stand-alone desalination plants, a technical study was carried out in the beginning of the project. A second study, after reasonable developments, has been carried out to discover the need and the social impact for potential users and to focus on the legal frame works and economic influences.

texts.label: page_header
texts.orig: H.E.S. Fath et al. / Desalination 225 (2008) 58-69
texts.text: H.E.S. Fath et al. / Desalination 225 (2008) 58-69

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The technical focus of the project is on the improvement of desalination modules based on membrane distillation (MD) for stand-alone systems. The MD technique is particularly suitable for maintenance-free and solar supplied systems. The membranes used have a high resistance against different water composites and operate in a temperature range of 60-90°C in which collectors  achieve  high  efficiency  and  good  performance. The developed technique was modular and covered a wide range of capacities (from 150 l/d up to 10 m 3 /d) so that the systems can be adapted to any particular demand.
texts.text: The technical focus of the project is on the improvement of desalination modules based on membrane distillation (MD) for stand-alone systems. The MD technique is particularly suitable for maintenance-free and solar supplied systems. The membranes used have a high resistance against different water composites and operate in a temperature range of 60-90°C in which collectors  achieve  high  efficiency  and  good  performance. The developed technique was modular and covered a wide range of capacities (from 150 l/d up to 10 m 3 /d) so that the systems can be adapted to any particular demand.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Two different types of MD modules as well as one type of heat exchangers were developed based on the same spiral-wound technology used for the MD modules. For very small and compact applications, the MD module was equipped with internal heat recovery. The aim was to achieve gained output ratios (GOR's) of 6-8 and low module pressure losses (0.2-0.6 bar) to reduce pump energy. For larger applications (larger than 1 m 3 /d) MD modules without internal heat recovery but with an external heat exchanger were used. The aim was to increase the module output (the output of modules without internal heat recovery can be two to three times higher), to decrease production costs and to increase the energy efficiency of the whole system (GOR's > 10) by connecting several MD modules to a single  high  efficiency  heat  exchanger.
texts.text: Two different types of MD modules as well as one type of heat exchangers were developed based on the same spiral-wound technology used for the MD modules. For very small and compact applications, the MD module was equipped with internal heat recovery. The aim was to achieve gained output ratios (GOR's) of 6-8 and low module pressure losses (0.2-0.6 bar) to reduce pump energy. For larger applications (larger than 1 m 3 /d) MD modules without internal heat recovery but with an external heat exchanger were used. The aim was to increase the module output (the output of modules without internal heat recovery can be two to three times higher), to decrease production costs and to increase the energy efficiency of the whole system (GOR's > 10) by connecting several MD modules to a single  high  efficiency  heat  exchanger.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3. Membrane distillation
texts.text: 3. Membrane distillation

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Membrane distillation (MD) is an innovative membrane technology [16-21]. Contrary to membranes for RO, MD membranes are hydrophobic. This means that up to a certain limiting pressure the  membrane cannot be wetted by liquid. In Fig. 1, the principal set-up for an MD module with internal  heat  recovery  is  sketched.  For  an  MD system, there is a lower temperature , for example 60°C, on one side of the membrane, then there exists water partial pressure difference between the  two  sides  of  the  membrane and thus water evaporates through the membrane. The water vapor condenses on the other side of the membrane and the distillate is formed. Some experience was gathered in recent experimental investigations with spiral wound MD modules with a basic setup as sketched in Fig. 2. The experiments show promising results.
texts.text: Membrane distillation (MD) is an innovative membrane technology [16-21]. Contrary to membranes for RO, MD membranes are hydrophobic. This means that up to a certain limiting pressure the  membrane cannot be wetted by liquid. In Fig. 1, the principal set-up for an MD module with internal  heat  recovery  is  sketched.  For  an  MD system, there is a lower temperature , for example 60°C, on one side of the membrane, then there exists water partial pressure difference between the  two  sides  of  the  membrane and thus water evaporates through the membrane. The water vapor condenses on the other side of the membrane and the distillate is formed. Some experience was gathered in recent experimental investigations with spiral wound MD modules with a basic setup as sketched in Fig. 2. The experiments show promising results.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The advantages of MD systems for stand-alone units were found to be:
texts.text: The advantages of MD systems for stand-alone units were found to be:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · The process works at low temperatures (6090°C) which is important for high efficiency of solar thermal collectors. (It is also possible to utilize waste heat from diesel engines or heat from co-generation plants.)
texts.text: The process works at low temperatures (6090°C) which is important for high efficiency of solar thermal collectors. (It is also possible to utilize waste heat from diesel engines or heat from co-generation plants.)

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Contrary to RO systems, no chemical pre-treatment of the feed water is necessary. Simple pre-filtration is sufficient.
texts.text: Contrary to RO systems, no chemical pre-treatment of the feed water is necessary. Simple pre-filtration is sufficient.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · The membranes are non-sensitive against algae, etc.
texts.text: The membranes are non-sensitive against algae, etc.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · The modules work at atmospheric pressure. It is not a vacuum technology as in MSF/MED or pressurized technology as in RO.
texts.text: The modules work at atmospheric pressure. It is not a vacuum technology as in MSF/MED or pressurized technology as in RO.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · The salinity of the feed water has almost no influence on the efficiency of the process.
texts.text: The salinity of the feed water has almost no influence on the efficiency of the process.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Membrane distillation produces very pure distillate.  The  electrical  conductivity  is  in  the range of 2-10 µS/cm.
texts.text: Membrane distillation produces very pure distillate.  The  electrical  conductivity  is  in  the range of 2-10 µS/cm.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Due to the modular set-up systems, a high range of water capacity units can be built.
texts.text: Due to the modular set-up systems, a high range of water capacity units can be built.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In the present SMADES project, MD technology was further developed to be applied and adopted to  the  conditions  for  solar  thermally driven desalination systems. A screening of suitable materials was carried out to achieve long time resistant systems. Control strategies for long term maintenance-free operation was developed. Lessons learned from earlier operating experience of solar thermally driven pilot plants with different distillation devices (for example multi effect humidification) showed that more emphasis has to be given to a well-developed total system design. To the present knowledge of the proposed project, the MD technology holds more development potential  than  other  thermally driven desalination techniques for small stand-alone systems.
texts.text: In the present SMADES project, MD technology was further developed to be applied and adopted to  the  conditions  for  solar  thermally driven desalination systems. A screening of suitable materials was carried out to achieve long time resistant systems. Control strategies for long term maintenance-free operation was developed. Lessons learned from earlier operating experience of solar thermally driven pilot plants with different distillation devices (for example multi effect humidification) showed that more emphasis has to be given to a well-developed total system design. To the present knowledge of the proposed project, the MD technology holds more development potential  than  other  thermally driven desalination techniques for small stand-alone systems.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Figs. 3 and 4 show the photo and the flow diagram of  Alexandria compact system, respectively. This system was installed and mounted on the roof of the Thermal Laboratory, Mechanical Engineering Department of Alexandria University, Alexandria, Egypt, in June 2005 and has been working properly since this time till now. The objective of installing this system was to carry-out experimental investigations with long-term maintenance-free operation under real weather conditions.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: section of the solar collector. From that storage tank the cold feed water is pumped to the condenser section of the MD module. The feed water is preheated using the latent heat of condensation of the distillate. The preheated feed water leaves the condenser and then enters the bottom section of the solar collector. The heat absorbed by the solar collector during the day time is transferred to  the  feed  water  (which  may  be  seawater,  or brackish water). The heated feed water leaves the collector area on the top, passes a degasser bottle to free it from gases and then enters the evaporator side of the MD module. Part of the heated feed water is evaporated through the membrane while the concentrated brine is recirculated back to the feed  storage  tank.  The  distillate  after  being condensated is either collected in a distillate tank

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Due to the separation of distillate, the water level in the storage tank decreases and the salt concentration in this tank increases as the brine comes back to this tank from the membrane module. To overcome the increase in salt concentration, the storage tank is refilled with fresh feed water from a well or another source of raw water. When the liquid level in the feed water storage tank drops to half the tank height, a switch activates the refilling pump and the storage tank is refilled with fresh feed water. The refilling process continues until the water level reaches about 90% of the storage tank height at which level another actuator switches the refilling pump off. The system is self-running since the pumps and all other electrical devices are supplied by the PV. The operation is only possible during day time because there is no other source of electricity used.
texts.text: Due to the separation of distillate, the water level in the storage tank decreases and the salt concentration in this tank increases as the brine comes back to this tank from the membrane module. To overcome the increase in salt concentration, the storage tank is refilled with fresh feed water from a well or another source of raw water. When the liquid level in the feed water storage tank drops to half the tank height, a switch activates the refilling pump and the storage tank is refilled with fresh feed water. The refilling process continues until the water level reaches about 90% of the storage tank height at which level another actuator switches the refilling pump off. The system is self-running since the pumps and all other electrical devices are supplied by the PV. The operation is only possible during day time because there is no other source of electricity used.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The solar collector consists of three rectangular similar sections connected in series. The dimensions of each section are 2020 mm × 1020 mm × 80 mm with a total area of 2.06 m 2 and an aperture area of 1.91 m 2 . Each collector section consists of 10 absorber finned tubes made from aluminium and connected in parallel to the header tubes. Each absorber fin has a width of 100 mm with a thickness of 2.0 mm. The aluminium finned tubing is equipped with Cu-Ni 10 tubes to resist the corrosion if seawater is used. This material withstands hot salt water and is often used in largescale desalination plants. The outer diameter of the riser tubes is 10 mm and the diameter of the header tubes is 20 mm.
texts.text: The solar collector consists of three rectangular similar sections connected in series. The dimensions of each section are 2020 mm × 1020 mm × 80 mm with a total area of 2.06 m 2 and an aperture area of 1.91 m 2 . Each collector section consists of 10 absorber finned tubes made from aluminium and connected in parallel to the header tubes. Each absorber fin has a width of 100 mm with a thickness of 2.0 mm. The aluminium finned tubing is equipped with Cu-Ni 10 tubes to resist the corrosion if seawater is used. This material withstands hot salt water and is often used in largescale desalination plants. The outer diameter of the riser tubes is 10 mm and the diameter of the header tubes is 20 mm.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The riser tubes are joined to the header tubes by brazing. In order to minimize the thermal contact resistance between the aluminium absorber profile and the Cu-Ni 10 riser tube, the riser tube is pushed into the open cylindrical back site of the aluminium profile, and then the tubular aluminium profile and the riser tube are grouted together. The sketch in Fig. 5 shows a cross section of the absorber profile. The four ends of the header tubes are passed through the collector casing to achieve a high flexibility with respect to collector field design and collector field interconnection.
texts.text: The riser tubes are joined to the header tubes by brazing. In order to minimize the thermal contact resistance between the aluminium absorber profile and the Cu-Ni 10 riser tube, the riser tube is pushed into the open cylindrical back site of the aluminium profile, and then the tubular aluminium profile and the riser tube are grouted together. The sketch in Fig. 5 shows a cross section of the absorber profile. The four ends of the header tubes are passed through the collector casing to achieve a high flexibility with respect to collector field design and collector field interconnection.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In order to achieve the operation temperatures of the membrane distillation system of 80-90°C, the solar thermal flat plate collector has to have a selectively coated absorber to reduce the heat loss by radiation. The absorber plate is made from aluminium. Its selective surface is applied by an electro-chemical process. The achieved characteristic values for the selective surface are 0.93 absorpitivity and 0.18 emissivity.
texts.text: In order to achieve the operation temperatures of the membrane distillation system of 80-90°C, the solar thermal flat plate collector has to have a selectively coated absorber to reduce the heat loss by radiation. The absorber plate is made from aluminium. Its selective surface is applied by an electro-chemical process. The achieved characteristic values for the selective surface are 0.93 absorpitivity and 0.18 emissivity.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The glass cover has a low iron content to reduce losses due to the absorption of radiation by iron particles. A low-iron glass cover is used to increase transmission to the absorber. The transmission of the solar radiation for glass with low iron is about 10% higher than for standard window glass with high iron content. The transmissivity of the glass used is 0.89.
texts.text: The glass cover has a low iron content to reduce losses due to the absorption of radiation by iron particles. A low-iron glass cover is used to increase transmission to the absorber. The transmission of the solar radiation for glass with low iron is about 10% higher than for standard window glass with high iron content. The transmissivity of the glass used is 0.89.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The measuring instrumentation and the data acquisition system were adapted to the different requirements of the compact system. So, the system which focuses on experimental investigations is highly equipped with sensors and with an electronic data acquisition system. Fig. 7 provides a list of all sensors and their locations in the hydraulic  loop. The  sketch  represents  the  highly equipped system for experimental investigations.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 5. Unit performance. Results and discussion
texts.text: 5. Unit performance. Results and discussion

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The unit is continuously running since its complete installation and collection of full measurements started in July 2005. The data are taken every 10 s for all temperatures, flow rates, electric conductivities and global solar radiation as indicated in Fig. 7. To show the performance, data for a clear day (17 August 2005) and for a cloudy day (30 October 2005) are presented. Fig. 8 shows the data collected for the clear day from sunrise to  sunset. The maximum solar intensity during this  day, I glob was 995 W/m 2  and the working evaporator inlet temperature was above 60°C. The electric conductivities for feed and distillate water were 526 and 3 µS/cm, respectively. The daily production was 64 l/d (11.2 l/m 2 d), while the ac-

The electric conductivities for feed and distillate water were 672 and 2 μ S/cm, respectively. The relation between the daily radiation and daily production is shown in Fig. 10. It is clear that the distillate daily production is directly proportional to the daily total radiation. A linear correlation

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 6. Concluding remarks
texts.text: 6. Concluding remarks

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. A  full  description  of  a  desalination  unit based on membrane distillation is given. The unit is self-operating using a PV panel to run the feed pump and solar collectors to heat up the feed water. Condensation energy is recovered in the condenser channel of the membrane to preheat the feed water.
texts.text: A  full  description  of  a  desalination  unit based on membrane distillation is given. The unit is self-operating using a PV panel to run the feed pump and solar collectors to heat up the feed water. Condensation energy is recovered in the condenser channel of the membrane to preheat the feed water.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2. A sample of the measurements for a clear day and a cloudy day is presented and indicates a high productivity of 11.2 l/m 2 .d for a total solar energy of 7.25 kWh/m 2 .d.
texts.text: A sample of the measurements for a clear day and a cloudy day is presented and indicates a high productivity of 11.2 l/m 2 .d for a total solar energy of 7.25 kWh/m 2 .d.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 3. A correlation between the unit productivity and total solar radiation is given.
texts.text: A correlation between the unit productivity and total solar radiation is given.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. The unit shows a high salt rejection performance as it reduced the electric conductivity of the feed water from 670 to about 3 µS/cm, for the
texts.text: The unit shows a high salt rejection performance as it reduced the electric conductivity of the feed water from 670 to about 3 µS/cm, for the

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: product. This gives a salt rejection percentage of about 99.5%.
texts.text: product. This gives a salt rejection percentage of about 99.5%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 5.The MD process efficiency is about 90% and the solar collector efficiency is about 50%.
texts.text: 5.The MD process efficiency is about 90% and the solar collector efficiency is about 50%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 6. The system is very suitable and promising for arid areas in the Arab and North African regions.
texts.text: The system is very suitable and promising for arid areas in the Arab and North African regions.
